findings.title: GitHub Event Expression in AI Action Input
findings.description: The AI action `openai/openai-github-action@v1` receives the GitHub event expression `${{ github.event.pull_request.body }}` via the `prompt` input parameter. While this expression may not be the highest-risk source, it could still carry attacker-controlled content (e.g. via forked repositories, external contributors, or social engineering).
findings.message: The AI action `openai/openai-github-action@v1` receives the GitHub event expression `${{ github.event.pull_request.body }}` via the `prompt` input parameter. While this expression may not be the highest-risk source, it could still carry attacker-controlled content (e.g. via forked repositories, external contributors, or social engineering).

findings.title: GitHub Event Expression in AI Action Input
findings.description: The AI action `anthropic-ai/claude-action@v2` receives the GitHub event expression `${{ github.event.pull_request.title }}` via the `prompt` input parameter. While this expression may not be the highest-risk source, it could still carry attacker-controlled content (e.g. via forked repositories, external contributors, or social engineering).
findings.message: The AI action `anthropic-ai/claude-action@v2` receives the GitHub event expression `${{ github.event.pull_request.title }}` via the `prompt` input parameter. While this expression may not be the highest-risk source, it could still carry attacker-controlled content (e.g. via forked repositories, external contributors, or social engineering).

findings.title: GitHub Event Expression in AI Action Input
findings.description: The AI action `langchain-ai/langchain-github-action@v1` receives the GitHub event expression `${{ github.event.issue.body }}` via the `issue-body` input parameter. While this expression may not be the highest-risk source, it could still carry attacker-controlled content (e.g. via forked repositories, external contributors, or social engineering).
findings.message: The AI action `langchain-ai/langchain-github-action@v1` receives the GitHub event expression `${{ github.event.issue.body }}` via the `issue-body` input parameter. While this expression may not be the highest-risk source, it could still carry attacker-controlled content (e.g. via forked repositories, external contributors, or social engineering).

findings.title: GitHub Event Expression in AI Action Input
findings.description: The AI action `langchain-ai/langchain-github-action@v1` receives the GitHub event expression `${{ github.event.comment.body }}` via the `comment-body` input parameter. While this expression may not be the highest-risk source, it could still carry attacker-controlled content (e.g. via forked repositories, external contributors, or social engineering).
findings.message: The AI action `langchain-ai/langchain-github-action@v1` receives the GitHub event expression `${{ github.event.comment.body }}` via the `comment-body` input parameter. While this expression may not be the highest-risk source, it could still carry attacker-controlled content (e.g. via forked repositories, external contributors, or social engineering).

findings.title: Issue/Comment Body in run Step
findings.description: The ``run`` step embeds `${{ github.event.issue.title }}`, which originates from user-submitted issue or comment content. Any GitHub user can craft issue bodies or comments containing prompt injection payloads to manipulate AI agents triggered by this workflow.
findings.message: The ``run`` step embeds `${{ github.event.issue.title }}`, which originates from user-submitted issue or comment content. Any GitHub user can craft issue bodies or comments containing prompt injection payloads to manipulate AI agents triggered by this workflow.

findings.title: Issue/Comment Body in run Step
findings.description: The ``run`` step embeds `${{ github.event.comment.body }}`, which originates from user-submitted issue or comment content. Any GitHub user can craft issue bodies or comments containing prompt injection payloads to manipulate AI agents triggered by this workflow.
findings.message: The ``run`` step embeds `${{ github.event.comment.body }}`, which originates from user-submitted issue or comment content. Any GitHub user can craft issue bodies or comments containing prompt injection payloads to manipulate AI agents triggered by this workflow.

findings.title: Issue/Comment Body in run Step
findings.description: The ``run`` step embeds `${{ github.event.issue.body }}`, which originates from user-submitted issue or comment content. Any GitHub user can craft issue bodies or comments containing prompt injection payloads to manipulate AI agents triggered by this workflow.
findings.message: The ``run`` step embeds `${{ github.event.issue.body }}`, which originates from user-submitted issue or comment content. Any GitHub user can craft issue bodies or comments containing prompt injection payloads to manipulate AI agents triggered by this workflow.
